CS Rolex CS focuses on creating the Rolex Picasso ceramic series. Transformed by German AET REMOULD, CS spent months meticulously researching and comparing every detail to produce the best and most elegant work. Specific diamond cutting tools and advanced six-axis CNC technology were used to overcome the technical challenges of ceramic fragility, resulting in flawless perfection. Hand polishing was applied to achieve exquisite detail and a beautiful gloss. The "units per hour" engraving on the bezel matches the dial color, with consistent white scale spacing and high-quality, clear font. The sapphire crystal glass mirror is pure and clear without coating, providing scratch resistance and enhancing the watch's overall quality. The ceramic Daytona retains the original features with slight modifications, such as a raised outer ring on the small seconds sub-dial for a more three-dimensional effect. The crown logo is meticulously crafted to be identical to the original, with smooth contours and a three-dimensional feel, unlike the rounded and rough edges of counterfeit logos. The single-gear movement makes time adjustment easier, and the hour and minute hands fit snugly against the dial, vividly recreating the racing experience. The ceramic case thickness matches the official specifications. CS's attention to detail ensures the best and most elegant results, offering customers a top-notch wearing experience.

"Sky Sunday Calendar Series" (m-) featuring the "True Meteorite" substitute literal, sourced from distant outer space, crafted from Swedish-imported Muonionalusta meteorite iron. Each piece is meticulously selected with a unique weave of iron and nickel in the "Widmanstatten" pattern, resulting in a high scrap rate from raw material selection to final product. Produced according to Swiss craftsmanship, it undergoes twenty processes to bid farewell to the visual fatigue of the original meteorite black and gray, revealing the most authentic genuine feel, highlighting the three major characteristics of white, bright, and shiny. This exquisite and unique meteorite literal brings a perfect visual feast, showcasing its rarity and most attractive feature - the unique patterns. Due to the slow vacuum cooling process over millions of years in space, meteorites form an octahedral meteorite iron structure (thus impossible to artificially synthesize on Earth), which is then cut into thin layers by processors. Only the slices meeting the standards are selected and undergo subsequent chemical acid treatment processes to become uniquely beautiful meteorite dials. Meteorites are used as a special material on watch dials not only for their preciousness but also because each meteorite dial, even with Widmanstatten patterns, forms a unique texture with intersecting short straight lines. This Rolex DAY-DATE weekly log-type F watch features a MM case, Cal automatic winding movement, date and calendar functions, and a meteorite dial with diamond markers. Seemingly ordinary, yet every watch with a meteorite dial becomes a "unique piece," representing high quality. Feel free to share!
